response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with genuine developer-tooling interest — including direct IDE development experience on NI LabWindows/CVI and multiple test-tooling projects — but the core stack does not match. His career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, with only incidental Java exposure and no evident Kotlin, Spring, or deep JVM experience, which are hard requirements for this role.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience (C/C++ components for LabWindows/CVI) and authored test runners/tooling libraries
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus teaching and workshop experience
Proven work on complex, high-scale systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby, large automation platforms)
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin experience and only minor, dated Java exposure — the role's primary languages
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM framework background
Recent work is full-stack web/AI tooling in TypeScript, far from JVM internals and application-server integration
